description: High-energy dance pop radio hit with infectious hooks, pulsing synths, and four-on-the-floor beats
suno_tags: dance pop, catchy hooks, pulsing synths, four-on-the-floor, energetic, female vocals, radio-ready, 124 BPM
vocal_style: Confident and commanding with a modern pop edge. Verses are rhythmic and cool, pre-chorus lifts with urgency, chorus explodes with full power. Clean tone with subtle electronic polish.

tips: Chorus must be singable after one listen — think 5-word hook max
Use short, punchy lines that match the beat pulse
Pre-chorus should create a lift — ascending energy in the words
Post-chorus can be a wordless hook or single repeated phrase
Write for the dance floor — body-movement words: move, feel, spin, light

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
Midnight and the floor is calling
Neon dripping down the wall
Feel the bass beneath my heartbeat
Tonight I'm giving it my all

[Pre-Chorus]
Lights flash, hands up
Don't you dare stop
Feel it rising, feel it climbing
Three, two, one

[Chorus]
We're electric, we're alive
Burn so bright we light the sky
Move your body, lose your mind
We're electric tonight

[Post-Chorus]
To-night, to-night, to-night
We're electric tonight

[Verse 2]
Strangers turning into family
Sweat and glitter, shared delight
Every song becomes a memory
We're writing with the light

[Pre-Chorus]
Lights flash, hands up
Don't you dare stop
Feel it rising, feel it climbing
Three, two, one

[Chorus]
We're electric, we're alive
Burn so bright we light the sky
Move your body, lose your mind
We're electric tonight

[Bridge]
[Break]
Shut it down, strip it back
Just the beat and the black
[Build]
Now bring it, bring it, bring it back

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
We're electric, we're alive
Burn so bright we light the sky
Move your body, lose your mind
We're electric tonight

[Outro]
We're electric tonight
[Effect: Fade Out]
[End]
do: Front-load the hook — first line of chorus IS the song title
Use a post-chorus for an infectious earworm moment
Strip the bridge down before bringing the final chorus back bigger
Keep total word count low — repetition is your friend
Write lines that feel good to shout in a crowd
dont: Don't write complex metaphors — dance pop is about feel, not poetry
Don't slow the tempo for the bridge — strip instruments but keep the pulse
Don't exceed 4 lines per section — brevity is king
Don't use sad or introspective language — this is pure energy
Don't forget the post-chorus — it's where the hook lives in dance pop
